source: CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
title: Ensure that S3 is configured with 'Block Public Access' enabled

input_row.source: CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure that S3 is configured with 'Block Public Access' enabled
input_row.description: Amazon S3 provides Block public access (bucket settings) and Block public access (account settings) to help you manage public access to Amazon S3 resources. By default, S3 buckets and objects are created with public access disabled. However, an IAM principal with sufficient S3 permissions can enable public access at the bucket and/or object level. While enabled, Block public access (bucket settings) prevents an individual bucket and its contained objects from becoming publicly accessible. Similarly, Block public access (account settings) prevents all buckets and their contained objects from becoming publicly accessible across the entire account.
input_row.rationale: Amazon S3 Block public access (bucket settings) prevents the accidental or malicious public exposure of data contained within the respective bucket(s). Amazon S3 Block public access (account settings) prevents the accidental or malicious public exposure of data contained within all buckets of the respective AWS account. Whether to block public access to all or some buckets is an organizational decision that should be based on data sensitivity, least privilege, and use case. Impact: When you apply Block Public Access settings to an account, the settings apply to all AWS regions globally. The settings may not take effect in all regions immediately or simultaneously, but they will eventually propagate to all regions.
input_row.audit: If utilizing Block Public Access (bucket settings) From Console: 1. Login to the AWS Management Console and open the Amazon S3 console using https://console.aws.amazon.com/s3/. 2. Select the check box next to a bucket. 3. Click on 'Edit public access settings'. 4. Ensure that the block public access settings are configured appropriately for this bucket. 5. Repeat for all the buckets in your AWS account. From Command Line: 1. List all of the S3 buckets: aws s3 ls 2. Find the public access settings for a specific bucket: aws s3api get-public-access-block --bucket <bucket-name> Output if Block Public Access is enabled: { "PublicAccessBlockConfiguration": { "BlockPublicAcls": true, "IgnorePublicAcls": true, "BlockPublicPolicy": true, "RestrictPublicBuckets": true } } If the output reads false for the separate configuration settings, then proceed with the remediation. If utilizing Block Public Access (account settings) From Console: 1. Login to the AWS Management Console and open the Amazon S3 console using https://console.aws.amazon.com/s3/. 2. Choose Block public access (account settings). 3. Ensure that the block public access settings are configured appropriately for your AWS account. From Command Line: To check the block public access settings for this account, run the following command: aws s3control get-public-access-block --account-id <account-id> -- region <region-name> Output if Block Public Access is enabled: { "PublicAccessBlockConfiguration": { "IgnorePublicAcls": true, "BlockPublicPolicy": true, "BlockPublicAcls": true, "RestrictPublicBuckets": true } } If the output reads false for the separate configuration settings, then proceed with the remediation.
input_row.remediation: If utilizing Block Public Access (bucket settings) From Console: 1. Login to the AWS Management Console and open the Amazon S3 console using https://console.aws.amazon.com/s3/. 2. Select the check box next to a bucket. 3. Click 'Edit public access settings'. 4. Click 'Block all public access' 5. Repeat for all the buckets in your AWS account that contain sensitive data. From Command Line: 1. List all of the S3 buckets: aws s3 ls 2. Enable Block Public Access on a specific bucket: aws s3api put-public-access-block --bucket <bucket-name> --public-access- block-configuration "BlockPublicAcls=true,IgnorePublicAcls=true,BlockPublicPolicy=true,RestrictPu blicBuckets=true" If utilizing Block Public Access (account settings) From Console: If the output reads true for the separate configuration settings, then Block Public Access is enabled on the account. 1. Login to the AWS Management Console and open the Amazon S3 console using https://console.aws.amazon.com/s3/. 2. Click Block Public Access (account settings). 3. Click Edit to change the block public access settings for all the buckets in your AWS account. 4. Update the settings and click Save. For details about each setting, pause on the i icons. 5. When you're asked for confirmation, enter confirm. Then click Confirm to save your changes. From Command Line: To enable Block Public Access for this account, run the following command: aws s3control put-public-access-block --public-access-block-configuration BlockPublicAcls=true, IgnorePublicAcls=true, BlockPublicPolicy=true, RestrictPublicBuckets=true --account-id <account-id>  References: 1. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/user-guide/block-public-access- account.html
input_row.impact: When you apply Block Public Access settings to an account, the settings apply to all AWS regions globally. The settings may not take effect in all regions immediately or simultaneously, but they will eventually propagate to all regions.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**

This compliance control requires checking the configuration of S3 "Block Public Access" settings at both the bucket and account levels. The audit information clearly mentions using AWS CLI commands such as `aws s3api get-public-access-block` for specific buckets and `aws s3control get-public-access-block` for account-level settings. These commands can programmatically verify the configuration via AWS APIs. Additionally, the remediation steps also include CLI commands for configuring these settings, confirming their accessibility through APIs. Therefore, this control fits well within the scope of what is generally automated following CSPM practices, leveraging AWS APIs to gather the necessary compliance data.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_s3_bucket_and_account_public_access_block_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**

1. **AWS API Calls/Boto3 Methods:**
   - Use `boto3.client('s3')` to access the S3 bucket settings.
   - Use `get_public_access_block(Bucket='<bucket-name>')` method to check "Block Public Access" settings for individual buckets.
   - Use `boto3.client('s3control')` for account-wide settings.
   - Use `get_public_access_block(AccountId='<account-id>')` method to check account-level public access blocks.

2. **AWS CLI Commands:**
   - `aws s3api get-public-access-block --bucket <bucket-name>` to verify individual bucket settings.
   - `aws s3control get-public-access-block --account-id <account-id>` to verify account-wide settings.

3. **Resources to Enumerate:**
   - Enumerate all S3 buckets within the account.
   - Check account-level settings for public access restrictions.

4. **Configuration Properties/Settings:**
   - Validate properties within `PublicAccessBlockConfiguration`:
     - `BlockPublicAcls`: should be `true`
     - `IgnorePublicAcls`: should be `true`
     - `BlockPublicPolicy`: should be `true`
     - `RestrictPublicBuckets`: should be `true`

5. **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
   - A bucket or account configuration passes if all aforementioned settings are set to `true`.
   - Any deviation from these settings will result in a non-compliant status.

6. **Example Check:**
   - Use `boto3.client('s3').get_public_access_block(Bucket='example-bucket')` and ensure the `PublicAccessBlockConfiguration` matches the expected secure configuration.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE

- The classification of this control as Automated is correct. The AWS CLI and boto3 provide the necessary capabilities to check the "Block Public Access" settings for both individual S3 buckets and at the account level. This aligns with CSPM practices that leverage AWS APIs for compliance checks.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 9

- The automation details are technically accurate. The use of `boto3.client('s3')` and `boto3.client('s3control')` for checking bucket and account-level settings is correct. The methods `get_public_access_block` for both bucket and account levels are valid.
- The AWS CLI commands provided are also accurate and correctly formatted.
- The program name `aws_s3_bucket_and_account_public_access_block_enabled` is descriptive but could be slightly improved for clarity.
- The pass/fail criteria are correctly defined, focusing on the four key settings that should be set to `true`.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**

- **Program Name:** Consider renaming the program to `aws_s3_public_access_block_check` for simplicity and clarity. This name succinctly describes the check being performed without being overly verbose.
- **Additional Considerations:** While the current approach is comprehensive, consider adding logging to capture the results of each check for auditing purposes. This can be done by integrating AWS CloudWatch Logs or using a centralized logging solution.
- **Alternative Methods:** While not necessary, AWS Config can be used to continuously monitor these settings. Config rules such as `s3-bucket-public-read-prohibited` and `s3-bucket-public-write-prohibited` can complement this audit by providing ongoing compliance monitoring.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

- The assessment is well-founded with accurate use of AWS APIs and CLI commands. The technical details are sound, and the approach aligns with AWS security best practices.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**

- **Modify:** Update the program name to `aws_s3_public_access_block_check` for improved clarity.
- The rest of the assessment is solid and should be kept as-is, given its technical correctness and alignment with compliance requirements.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identify that AWS provides full API and CLI support for programmatically checking S3 "Block Public Access" settings at both the bucket and account levels. The required properties are directly accessible via boto3 (`get_public_access_block` for both `s3` and `s3control` clients) and AWS CLI, making this control fully automatable. No manual inspection is required, and all compliance criteria can be validated through API calls.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
aws_s3_bucket_and_account_block_public_access_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- AWS Resource/Service: S3 Buckets, S3 Account-level Settings
- API/CLI Method:
  - For buckets: `boto3.client('s3').get_public_access_block(Bucket='<bucket-name>')` or `aws s3api get-public-access-block --bucket <bucket-name>`
  - For account: `boto3.client('s3control').get_public_access_block(AccountId='<account-id>')` or `aws s3control get-public-access-block --account-id <account-id>`
- Properties to Check:
  - `BlockPublicAcls`
  - `IgnorePublicAcls`
  - `BlockPublicPolicy`
  - `RestrictPublicBuckets`
- Pass Criteria:
  - All four properties are set to `True` for every S3 bucket and at the account level.
- Fail Criteria:
  - Any of the four properties are set to `False` for any S3 bucket or at the account level.

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
There were no substantive disagreements between the two AI opinions; both supported automation. The only minor difference was in program naming. I selected a program name that is both descriptive and consistent with AWS compliance automation naming conventions.

**SOURCE:** CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
